response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling passion and even direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI), but his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and full-stack web work. The role's hard requirements — strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience — are essentially absent: Java appears only as a peripheral skill, and Kotlin and Spring are never mentioned. Despite impressive breadth and big-company credentials, the core stack mismatch is too large for a senior JVM-tooling IC role.
strengths: Direct IDE internals experience building components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Proven work on complex, large-scale, low-latency systems at major companies
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only peripheral, dated Java exposure
No Spring or enterprise JVM framework background
Primary expertise (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang) diverges from the JVM-centric stack
